frozen_components: embedding matrices (read for soft concept construction)
architecture.summary: SCM is a training framework that constructs soft concept vectors as probability-weighted averages of token embeddings at each decoding step, mixes these vectors into the model's hidden states via addition, and optimizes the resulting latent reasoning policy using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO). The soft concept vector serves as a continuous guidance signal that exposes the model during training to the soft representations it uses during reasoning, bridging the gap between discrete-token training and continuous-space inference.
trained_components: LoRA adapter (rank=32, alpha=64) on attention and MLP layers
latent policy combining soft concept mixing and token sampling

biblio.title: Improving Latent Reasoning in LLMs via Soft Concept Mixing

claims.statement: SCM improves performance over GRPO baseline on DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B, achieving 94.40% on MATH500 vs. 93.20% for GRPO and 91.80% for CoT baseline
On AIME 2024, SCM achieves 56.67% accuracy on DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B, matching GRPO but outperforming Soft Thinking (46.67%)
Average performance across five reasoning benchmarks (MATH500, AIME 2024, GSM8K, GPQA-Diamond, MMLU) is 72.32% for SCM vs. 71.65% for GRPO on DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B
SCM maintains consistent gains on smaller models; on DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1.5B, SCM achieves 55.00% average accuracy vs. 54.19% for GRPO
Ablation study shows hidden state fusion is necessary; removing it (SCM w/o hidden states) reduces DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B average accuracy to 71.37% from 72.32%
PCA analysis of hidden states reveals only slight latent shifts comparable to GRPO baseline, indicating stable training dynamics without significant representational drift

SCM resolves the train-inference mismatch by exposing the model to soft concept vectors during training, whereas prior work Soft Thinking applies continuous reasoning only at inference time without training exposure
SCM exhibits higher and more stable rewards compared to GRPO in later training stages, as shown in training dynamics analysis
